label: Formula A1.1
nearby_text: Consider a continuous random variable, $ x_t $, indexed by continuous time, $ t $. If $ \delta_x = x_{t+\delta_t} - x_t $ (the change in $ x_t $ over a very small time interval, $ \delta_t $) satisfies then $x_t$ is said to be a diffusion process, provided the additional technical restriction that $x_t$ is a Markov process (the transition probabilities depend only on the current value of the process and no other aspects of its history) is satisfied. The functions $m(x)$ and $v(x)$ are defined shortly, while the notation $o(\delta_t)$ means that any remaining terms are of order $\delta_t^2$ (or higher), and hence small relative to $\delta_t$. Formally, $\lim_{\delta_t \to 0} o(\delta_t)/\delta_t = 0$, so we can ignore terms of order $\delta_t^2$ (or higher) when $\delta_t$ itself is very small. Likewise, the notation $O(x)$ means terms of order $x$, and so for example, $O(x^2) = o(x)$.

subsection: Derivation of the Kolmogorov Forward Equation
nearby_text: For completeness, we present a derivation of Equation A1.6, using the diffusion-approximation assumptions (Equations A1.1a–A1.1c). This section is a bit technical and can be skipped if readers desire. Consider the change from the probability distribution, $ \varphi(x, t, p) $, at time $ t $ to a new distribution, $ \varphi(x, t + \delta_t, p) $, after some very small time interval, $ \delta_t $. To arrive at $ x $ at time $ t + \delta_t $, the frequency must have previously been at some value, $ x - \delta_x $, and then moved by an amount, $ \delta_x $, over the interval $ \delta_t $. Let $ \phi(\delta_x, x, \delta_t) $ be the probability of jumping by an amount, $ \delta_x $, over the time $ \delta_t $ given the starting point, $ x $. In our case, because we are assuming that there is a move from $ x - \delta_x $ to $ x $, we consider $ \phi(\delta_x, x - \delta_x, \delta_t) $. Integrating over all possible jump values yields the Chapman-Kolmogorov equation

label: Formula A1.26
nearby_text: While the KFE provides both the full solution of $ \varphi(x, t, p) $ and, where appropriate, the equilibrium solution, we can obtain much simpler expressions for many quantities of interest when an equilibrium solution does not exist. For example, if one or both of the boundaries are accessible and absorbing, we can compute the fixation probabilities (the probability that the process eventually will reach a specified boundary), the time to reach that boundary (the time to loss or fixation), and the expected value of many other functions of interest. The key to all of these operations is the Kolmogorov backward equation (KBE), which is given by The KBE is derived in a manner similar to the KFE. The KBE starts at the current time, t, and looks backwards in terms of how changes $ \left(\partial/\partial p\right) $ in the initial starting value, p, are influenced by the current position, x, hence the name. Conversely, the forward equation (Equation A1.6) examines how changes $ \left(\partial/\partial x\right) $ in the current position, x, are influenced by the starting value, p.

  "nearby_text": "While the KFE provides both the full solution of $ \\varphi(x, t, p) $ and, where appropriate, the equilibrium solution, we can obtain much simpler expressions for many quantities of interest when an equilibrium solution does not exist. For example, if one or both of the boundaries are accessible and absorbing, we can compute the fixation probabilities (the probability that the process eventually will reach a specified boundary), the time to reach that boundary (the time to loss or fixation), and the expected value of many other functions of interest. The key to all of these operations is the Kolmogorov backward equation (KBE), which is given by The KBE is derived in a manner similar to the KFE. The KBE starts at the current time, t, and looks backwards in terms of how changes $ \\left(\\partial/\\partial p\\right) $ in the initial starting value, p, are influenced by the current position, x, hence the name. Conversely, the forward equation (Equation A1.6) examines how changes $ \\left(\\partial/\\partial x\\right) $ in the current position, x, are influenced by the starting value, p."
}

label: Formula A1.27
nearby_text: When at least one boundary is absorbing (and accessible), no stationary distribution exists. In such cases, one important descriptor of the process is the probability of reaching one boundary before the other. One can show that the function $ u(p, t) $, the probability of fixation by time t, given that we start at allele frequency p, satisfies the KBE. We are typically interested in the ultimate probability of fixation, $ u(p) = \lim_{t \to \infty} u(p, t) $, in which case the partial derivative of $ u(p) $ with respect to time is zero and, from Equation A1.16, $ u(p) $ satisfies

label: Formula A1.30
nearby_text: The time for a process to reach a specified value (or values) is called the sojourn time. While we are often interested in the sojourn time to an absorbing boundary (the fixation time), the more general problem of the time to first reach a specific value within the interval over which the diffusion is defined is also tractable. Letting $ \bar{t}_{a,b} $ denote the expected time that a diffusion spends in the interval $ (a,b) $ given it starts at frequency p, then

  "nearby_text": "The time for a process to reach a specified value (or values) is called the sojourn time. While we are often interested in the sojourn time to an absorbing boundary (the fixation time), the more general problem of the time to first reach a specific value within the interval over which the diffusion is defined is also tractable. Letting $ \\bar{t}_{a,b} $ denote the expected time that a diffusion spends in the interval $ (a,b) $ given it starts at frequency p, then"

nearby_text: Finally, we note that $ \bar{t}, \bar{t}_{F} $, and $ \bar{t}_{L} $ are related by That is, the expected time to loss or fixation is equal to the expected time to fixation multiplied by the probability of fixation plus the expected time to loss multiplied by the probability of loss.

        "latex": "h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&\\frac{2[1-u(p)]}{v(x)G(x)}\\int_{a}^{x}G(y)dy&\\text{for}a<x<p\\\\ &\\frac{2u(p)}{v(x)G(x)}\\int_{x}^{b}G(y)dy&\\text{for}p<x<b\\end{aligned}\\right.",

label: Formula A1.41
nearby_text: These results were first obtained by Kimura and Ohta (1969a, 1969b). As a check of consistency, we can apply Equation A1.24, which (as expected) recovers our expression (Equation A1.25) for the unconditional time t.

        "latex": "\\bar{t}_{a,b}=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\Pr\\left(a\\leq x_{t}\\leq b\\right)dt=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\int_{a}^{b}\\varphi(x,t,p)dx dt",

        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 37,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",

        ]

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.39",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{L}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{0}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\int_{0}^{p}dx+4N_{e}\\frac{p}{1-p}\\int_{p}^{1}\\frac{1-x}{x}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\right)\\ln(p)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.39",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",

        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 42,

        "label": "Formula A1.44",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.37",
        "latex": "h_{0}(x,p)=\\frac{1-x}{1-p}h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&4N_{e}&&for0<x<p\\\\ &4N_{e}\\frac{p(1-x)}{x(1-p)}&&for p<x<1\\end{aligned}\\right.",

        "latex": "h_{1}(x,p)=\\frac{x}{p}h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&4N_{e}\\frac{x(1-p)}{p(1-x)}&for0<x<p\\\\ &4N_{e}&for p<x<1\\end{aligned}\\right.",

        "id": "formula_A1.39",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{L}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{0}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\int_{0}^{p}dx+4N_{e}\\frac{p}{1-p}\\int_{p}^{1}\\frac{1-x}{x}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\right)\\ln(p)\\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{F}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{1}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\frac{1-p}{p}\\int_{0}^{p}\\frac{x}{1-x}dx+4N_{e}\\int_{p}^{1}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\right)\\ln(1-p)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.40",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.41",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}&=u(p)\\bar{t}_{F}(p)+\\left[1-u(p)\\right]\\bar{t}_{L}(p)\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left[p\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\ln(1-p)\\right)+(1-p)\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\ln(p)\\right)\\right]\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left[(1-p)\\ln(1-p)+p\\ln(p)\\right]\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.41",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

      },
      {

label: Formula A1.50
nearby_text: For Brownian motion (also called the Wiener process), the diffusion over the interval $ -\infty < x < \infty $ is defined as where b > 0. The general solution under Brownian motion starting at $ x_{0} $ is that namely, the distribution of $ x_t $ is normal, with a mean of $ x_0 + at $ and a variance of $ \sigma_t^2 = bt $. There is no equilibrium solution, as the process converges to a normal distribution with infinite variance (and, if $ a \neq 0 $, an infinite mean).

  "nearby_text": "For Brownian motion (also called the Wiener process), the diffusion over the interval $ -\\infty < x < \\infty $ is defined as where b > 0. The general solution under Brownian motion starting at $ x_{0} $ is that namely, the distribution of $ x_t $ is normal, with a mean of $ x_0 + at $ and a variance of $ \\sigma_t^2 = bt $. There is no equilibrium solution, as the process converges to a normal distribution with infinite variance (and, if $ a \\neq 0 $, an infinite mean)."

label: Formula A1.51
nearby_text: For Brownian motion (also called the Wiener process), the diffusion over the interval $ -\infty < x < \infty $ is defined as where b > 0. The general solution under Brownian motion starting at $ x_{0} $ is that namely, the distribution of $ x_t $ is normal, with a mean of $ x_0 + at $ and a variance of $ \sigma_t^2 = bt $. There is no equilibrium solution, as the process converges to a normal distribution with infinite variance (and, if $ a \neq 0 $, an infinite mean).

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.8",
        "latex": "f(x-a)=f(x)-a\\frac{\\partial f(x)}{\\partial x}+\\frac{a^{2}}{2}\\frac{\\partial^{2}f(x)}{\\partial x^{2}}-\\frac{a^{3}}{6}\\frac{\\partial^{3}f(x)}{\\partial x^{3}}+o(a^{3})",
        "label": "Formula A1.8",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",
        "subsection": "Derivation of the Kolmogorov Forward Equation",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.9",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\varphi(x-\\delta_{x},t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x-\\delta_{x},\\delta_{t})&=\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})-\\delta_{x}\\frac{\\partial\\big[\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})\\big]}{\\partial x}\\\\&+\\frac{\\delta_{x}^{2}}{2}\\frac{\\partial^{2}\\big[\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})\\big]}{\\partial x^{2}}-\\frac{\\delta_{x}^{3}}{6}\\frac{\\partial^{3}\\big[\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})\\big]}{\\partial x^{3}}+o(\\delta_{x}^{3})\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A1.9",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",
        "subsection": "Derivation of the Kolmogorov Forward Equation",
        "position": 8,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "\\phi",
          "\\varphi",
          "t",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.10",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\int\\varphi(x-\\delta_{x},t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x-\\delta_{x},\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}&\\simeq\\int\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}-\\int\\delta_{x}\\frac{\\partial[\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})]}{\\partial x}d\\delta_{x}\\\\+\\int\\frac{\\delta_{x}^{2}}{2}\\frac{\\partial^{2}[\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})]}{\\partial x^{2}}d\\delta_{x}-\\int\\frac{\\delta_{x}^{3}}{6}\\frac{\\partial^{3}[\\varphi(x,t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})]}{\\partial x^{3}}d\\delta_{x}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A1.10",

        "subsection": "Derivation of the Kolmogorov Forward Equation",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.11",
        "latex": "\\int\\varphi(x-\\delta_{x},t)\\phi(\\delta_{x},x-\\delta_{x},\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}=\\varphi(x,t)\\int\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}-\\frac{\\partial}{\\partial x}\\left(\\varphi(x,t)\\int\\delta_{x}\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}\\right)",
        "label": "Formula A1.11",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",

      },
      {

        "latex": "+\\frac{1}{2}\\frac{\\partial^{2}}{\\partial x^{2}}\\left(\\varphi(x,t)\\int\\delta_{x}^{2}\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}\\right)-\\frac{1}{6}\\frac{\\partial^{3}}{\\partial x^{3}}\\left(\\varphi(x,t)\\int\\delta_{x}^{3}\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}\\right)",
        "label": "Formula A1.12",

      {

        ]

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.14",
        "latex": "\\int\\delta_{x}\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}=E\\left(\\delta_{x}\\mid x_{t}=x\\right)=m(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})",
        "label": "Formula A1.14",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.15",
        "latex": "\\int\\delta_{x}^{2}\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}=\\sigma^{2}(\\delta_{x})+[E(\\delta_{x})]^{2}",
        "label": "Formula A1.15",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",

        "id": "formula_A1.16",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\int\\delta_{x}^{2}\\phi(\\delta_{x},x,\\delta_{t})d\\delta_{x}&=[v(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})]+[m(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})]^{2}\\\\&=v(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A1.16",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",
        "subsection": "Derivation of the Kolmogorov Forward Equation",
        "position": 15,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.17",
        "latex": "\\varphi(x,t+\\delta_{t})=\\varphi(x,t)-\\frac{\\partial\\left\\{\\left[m(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})\\right]\\varphi(x,t)\\right\\}}{\\partial x}+\\frac{1}{2}\\frac{\\partial^{2}\\left\\{\\left[v(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})\\right]\\varphi(x,t)\\right\\}}{\\partial x^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula A1.17",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.18",
        "latex": "\\lim_{\\delta_{t}\\to0}\\frac{\\varphi(x,t+\\delta_{t})-\\varphi(x,t)}{\\delta_{t}}=\\frac{\\partial\\varphi(x,t)}{\\partial t}",
        "label": "Formula A1.18",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",
        "subsection": "Derivation of the Kolmogorov Forward Equation",
        "position": 17,

      {

        "latex": "\\lim_{\\delta_{t}\\to0}\\frac{1}{\\delta_{t}}\\left(\\frac{\\partial\\left\\{\\left[m(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})\\right]\\varphi(x,t)\\right\\}}{\\partial x}\\right)=\\frac{\\partial[m(x)\\varphi(x,t)]}{\\partial x}",
        "label": "Formula A1.19",

      {

        "latex": "\\lim_{\\delta_{t}\\to0}\\frac{1}{\\delta_{t}}\\left(\\frac{\\partial^{2}\\left\\{\\left[v(x)\\delta_{t}+o(\\delta_{t})\\right]\\varphi(x,t)\\right\\}}{\\partial x^{2}}\\right)=\\frac{\\partial^{2}[v(x)\\varphi(x,t)]}{\\partial x^{2}}",

        "label": "Formula A1.21",

      {

        "latex": "\\varphi(x)=\\frac{C}{v(x)G(x)}",
        "label": "Formula A1.25",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.26",
        "latex": "\\frac{\\partial\\varphi(x,t,p)}{\\partial t}=\\frac{1}{2}v(p)\\frac{\\partial^{2}\\varphi(x,t,p)}{\\partial p^{2}}+m(p)\\frac{\\partial\\varphi(x,t,p)}{\\partial p}",
        "label": "Formula A1.26",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.27",
        "latex": "0=m(p)\\frac{\\partial u(p)}{\\partial p}+\\frac{1}{2}v(p)\\frac{\\partial^{2}u(p)}{\\partial p^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula A1.27",

        "subsection": "Probability of Fixation",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.28",
        "latex": "u(p)=\\frac{\\int_{0}^{p}G(x)dx}{\\int_{0}^{1}G(x)dx}",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.29",
        "latex": "u_{b,a}(p)=\\frac{\\int_{a}^{p}G(x)dx}{\\int_{a}^{b}G(x)dx}",
        "label": "Formula A1.29",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Probability of Fixation",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.30",
        "latex": "\\bar{t}_{a,b}=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\Pr\\left(a\\leq x_{t}\\leq b\\right)dt=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\int_{a}^{b}\\varphi(x,t,p)dx dt",
        "label": "Formula A1.30",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.31",
        "latex": "h(x,p)=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\varphi(x,t,p)dt",
        "label": "Formula A1.31",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 30,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.32",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\bar t_{a,b}=\\int_a^b h(x,p)dx\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A1.32",

        "symbols_defined": [

          "a",
          "b"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.33",
        "latex": "h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&\\frac{2[1-u(p)]}{v(x)G(x)}\\int_{a}^{x}G(y)dy&\\text{for}a<x<p\\\\ &\\frac{2u(p)}{v(x)G(x)}\\int_{x}^{b}G(y)dy&\\text{for}p<x<b\\end{aligned}\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A1.33",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",

        ]

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.34",
        "latex": "h_{1}(x,p)=h(x,p)\\frac{u(x)}{u(p)}",

      {

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.36",
        "latex": "\\bar{t}(p)=u(p)\\bar{t}_{F}(p)+[1-u(p)]\\bar{t}_{L}(p)",
        "label": "Formula A1.36",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",

          "\\bar{t}",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.37",
        "latex": "h_{0}(x,p)=\\frac{1-x}{1-p}h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&4N_{e}&&for0<x<p\\\\ &4N_{e}\\frac{p(1-x)}{x(1-p)}&&for p<x<1\\end{aligned}\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A1.37",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",

        ]

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.38",
        "latex": "h_{1}(x,p)=\\frac{x}{p}h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&4N_{e}\\frac{x(1-p)}{p(1-x)}&for0<x<p\\\\ &4N_{e}&for p<x<1\\end{aligned}\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A1.38",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.39",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{L}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{0}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\int_{0}^{p}dx+4N_{e}\\frac{p}{1-p}\\int_{p}^{1}\\frac{1-x}{x}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\right)\\ln(p)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.39",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.40",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{F}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{1}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\frac{1-p}{p}\\int_{0}^{p}\\frac{x}{1-x}dx+4N_{e}\\int_{p}^{1}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\right)\\ln(1-p)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.40",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 39,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "F",
          "\\bar{t}",
          "p"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.41",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}&=u(p)\\bar{t}_{F}(p)+\\left[1-u(p)\\right]\\bar{t}_{L}(p)\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left[p\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\ln(1-p)\\right)+(1-p)\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\ln(p)\\right)\\right]\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left[(1-p)\\ln(1-p)+p\\ln(p)\\right]\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.41",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 40,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\bar{t}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.42",
        "latex": "(1-p)/p\\simeq1/p=2N,\\quad p/(1-p)\\simeq p=1/(2N)",

        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.43",
        "latex": "\\ln(1-p)\\simeq-p=-1/(2N),\\quad\\ln(p)=-\\ln(1/p)=-\\ln(2N)",
        "label": "Formula A1.43",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 42,
        "symbols_defined": [

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}&=-4N_{e}[\\left(1-p\\right)\\ln(1-p)+p\\ln(p)]\\\\&\\simeq-4N_{e}\\left[-1/(2N)-\\ln(2N)/(2N)\\right]\\simeq\\frac{2N_{e}}{N}\\ln(2N)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.44",

        "latex": "\\frac{\\partial^{2}[v(x)\\varphi(x)]}{2\\partial x^{2}}=\\frac{\\partial[m(x)\\varphi(x)]}{\\partial x}",

      {

        "label": "Formula A1.24",

        "subsection": "Stationary Distributions",
        "position": 23,

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.25",
        "latex": "\\varphi(x)=\\frac{C}{v(x)G(x)}",
        "label": "Formula A1.25",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",
        "subsection": "Stationary Distributions",
        "position": 24,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.26",
        "latex": "\\frac{\\partial\\varphi(x,t,p)}{\\partial t}=\\frac{1}{2}v(p)\\frac{\\partial^{2}\\varphi(x,t,p)}{\\partial p^{2}}+m(p)\\frac{\\partial\\varphi(x,t,p)}{\\partial p}",
        "label": "Formula A1.26",
        "section": "FOUNDATIONS OF DIFFUSION THEORY",
        "subsection": "The Kolmogorov Backward Equation",
        "position": 25,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\varphi",
          "p",
          "t",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.27",
        "latex": "0=m(p)\\frac{\\partial u(p)}{\\partial p}+\\frac{1}{2}v(p)\\frac{\\partial^{2}u(p)}{\\partial p^{2}}",
        "label": "Formula A1.27",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Probability of Fixation",
        "position": 26,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "m"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.28",
        "latex": "u(p)=\\frac{\\int_{0}^{p}G(x)dx}{\\int_{0}^{1}G(x)dx}",
        "label": "Formula A1.28",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Probability of Fixation",
        "position": 27,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.29",
        "latex": "u_{b,a}(p)=\\frac{\\int_{a}^{p}G(x)dx}{\\int_{a}^{b}G(x)dx}",
        "label": "Formula A1.29",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Probability of Fixation",
        "position": 28,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.30",
        "latex": "\\bar{t}_{a,b}=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\Pr\\left(a\\leq x_{t}\\leq b\\right)dt=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\int_{a}^{b}\\varphi(x,t,p)dx dt",
        "label": "Formula A1.30",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 29,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.31",
        "latex": "h(x,p)=\\int_{0}^{\\infty}\\varphi(x,t,p)dt",
        "label": "Formula A1.31",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 30,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",
          "p",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.32",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\bar t_{a,b}=\\int_a^b h(x,p)dx\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A1.32",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 31,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\bar{t}",
          "a",
          "b"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.33",
        "latex": "h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&\\frac{2[1-u(p)]}{v(x)G(x)}\\int_{a}^{x}G(y)dy&\\text{for}a<x<p\\\\ &\\frac{2u(p)}{v(x)G(x)}\\int_{x}^{b}G(y)dy&\\text{for}p<x<b\\end{aligned}\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A1.33",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 32,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",
          "p",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.34",
        "latex": "h_{1}(x,p)=h(x,p)\\frac{u(x)}{u(p)}",
        "label": "Formula A1.34",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 33,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",
          "p",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.35",
        "latex": "h_{0}(x,p)=h(x,p)\\frac{1-u(x)}{1-u(p)}",
        "label": "Formula A1.35",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 34,
        "symbols_defined": [

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.36",
        "latex": "\\bar{t}(p)=u(p)\\bar{t}_{F}(p)+[1-u(p)]\\bar{t}_{L}(p)",
        "label": "Formula A1.36",
        "section": "DIFFUSION APPLICATIONS IN POPULATION GENETICS",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 35,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\bar{t}",
          "p"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.37",
        "latex": "h_{0}(x,p)=\\frac{1-x}{1-p}h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&4N_{e}&&for0<x<p\\\\ &4N_{e}\\frac{p(1-x)}{x(1-p)}&&for p<x<1\\end{aligned}\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A1.37",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 36,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",
          "p",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.38",
        "latex": "h_{1}(x,p)=\\frac{x}{p}h(x,p)=\\left\\{\\begin{aligned}&4N_{e}\\frac{x(1-p)}{p(1-x)}&for0<x<p\\\\ &4N_{e}&for p<x<1\\end{aligned}\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A1.38",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 37,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "h",
          "p",
          "x"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.39",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{L}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{0}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\int_{0}^{p}dx+4N_{e}\\frac{p}{1-p}\\int_{p}^{1}\\frac{1-x}{x}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\right)\\ln(p)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.39",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 38,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "L",
          "\\bar{t}",
          "p"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.40",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}_{F}(p)&=\\int_{0}^{1}h_{1}(x,p)dx=4N_{e}\\frac{1-p}{p}\\int_{0}^{p}\\frac{x}{1-x}dx+4N_{e}\\int_{p}^{1}dx\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\right)\\ln(1-p)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.40",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 39,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "F",
          "\\bar{t}",
          "p"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.41",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}&=u(p)\\bar{t}_{F}(p)+\\left[1-u(p)\\right]\\bar{t}_{L}(p)\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left[p\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\ln(1-p)\\right)+(1-p)\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\ln(p)\\right)\\right]\\\\&=-4N_{e}\\left[(1-p)\\ln(1-p)+p\\ln(p)\\right]\\\\ \\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.41",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 40,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\bar{t}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.42",
        "latex": "(1-p)/p\\simeq1/p=2N,\\quad p/(1-p)\\simeq p=1/(2N)",
        "label": "Formula A1.42",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 41,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "p"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.43",
        "latex": "\\ln(1-p)\\simeq-p=-1/(2N),\\quad\\ln(p)=-\\ln(1/p)=-\\ln(2N)",
        "label": "Formula A1.43",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 42,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\ln",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.44",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\bar{t}&=-4N_{e}[\\left(1-p\\right)\\ln(1-p)+p\\ln(p)]\\\\&\\simeq-4N_{e}\\left[-1/(2N)-\\ln(2N)/(2N)\\right]\\simeq\\frac{2N_{e}}{N}\\ln(2N)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula A1.44",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",
        "position": 43,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\bar{t}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A1.45",
        "latex": "\\bar{t}_{L}=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{p}{1-p}\\right)\\ln(p)\\simeq\\frac{2N_{e}}{N}\\ln(2N)",
        "label": "Formula A1.45",
        "section": "Appendix: Introduction",
        "subsection": "Time to Fixation",

      {
        "id": "formula_A1.46",
        "latex": "\\bar{t}_{F}=-4N_{e}\\left(\\frac{1-p}{p}\\right)\\ln(1-p)\\simeq-4N_{e}(2N)\\left(-\\frac{1}{2N}\\right)=4N_{e}",
        "label": "Formula A1.46",

  "nearby_text": "See Karlin and Taylor (1981) for a derivation. The resulting stationary distribution is normal with a mean of zero (on the assumed scale) and a variance of $ b/(2a) $.
